question: Q.1 To **'tilt at windmills'** means to:
explanation:
• **Fight imaginary enemies**: Derived from Don Quixote, who attacked windmills thinking they were giants. (Related: Idealistic but impractical). ⚔️🌬️
• **Waste energy**: To spend effort on something useless. (Related: Flog a dead horse). 🐎

question: Q.2 What does **'a swan song'** refer to?
explanation:
• **Final performance**: Based on the ancient belief that swans sing a beautiful song just before they die. (Related: Farewell appearance). 🦢🎶
• **A cry for help**: An appeal made in a difficult situation. (Related: S.O.S). 🆘

question: Q.3 **'Burning the midnight oil'** implies:
options: Working or studying late at night
answer: Working or studying late at night
explanation:
• **Working late**: To stay up late to finish a task, referring to the time before electricity when oil lamps were used. (Related: Night owl). 🏮📚
• **Wasting resources**: Using things up needlessly. (Related: Squandering). 💸

question: Q.4 A **'litmus test'** is a:
explanation:
• **Decisive test**: A single factor or event that proves the true nature or quality of something. (Related: Acid test). 🧪⚖️
• **Eye test**: A medical examination of vision. (Related: Optometry). 👁️

question: Q.5 If you can't **'hold a candle to'** someone, you are:
explanation:
• **Not as good**: Being significantly inferior to someone else in skill or quality. (Related: No match for). 🕯️📉
• **Helping them**: Assisting another person. (Related: Lending a hand). 🤝

question: Q.6 To **'take the bull by the horns'** is to:
explanation:
• **Face directly**: To deal with a difficult situation in a very direct and confident way. (Related: Confront). 🐂🏽
• **Avoid danger**: Staying away from risk. (Related: Play it safe). 🛡️

question: Q.7 A **'Pyrrhic victory'** is a victory that:
options: Comes at too high a cost
answer: Comes at too high a cost
explanation:
• **High cost**: A success that is not worth winning because so much is lost to achieve it. (Related: Hollow victory). ⚔️📉
• **Won easily**: Achieving success without effort. (Related: Hands down). 🙌

question: Q.8 To **'leave no stone unturned'** means to:
explanation:
• **Every possible way**: To make every effort to find something or solve a problem. (Related: Thorough investigation). 🪨🔍
• **Build a wall**: Constructing a barrier. (Related: Fortify). 🧱

question: Q.9 To **'throw down the gauntlet'** is to:
explanation:
• **Issue a challenge**: In medieval times, throwing a glove (gauntlet) on the ground was a way of challenging someone to a duel. (Related: Defy). 🧤⚔️
• **Accept defeat**: Giving up in a conflict. (Related: Throw in the towel). 🏳️

question: Q.10 Offering an **'olive branch'** means:
explanation:
• **Gesture of peace**: An offer to end a disagreement or conflict. (Related: Reconciliation). 🕊️🌿
• **Starting a fight**: Initiating a quarrel. (Related: Pick a fight). 🤺

question: Q.11 To **'steer clear of'** someone is to:
explanation:
• **Avoid them**: To stay away from a person or place because they are dangerous or unpleasant. (Related: Shun). ⛵🚫
• **Be their friend**: Establishing a good relationship. (Related: Get along). 🤝

question: Q.12 To **'keep something at bay'** means to:
explanation:
• **Keep away**: To prevent someone or something unpleasant from harming you or coming too close. (Related: Ward off). 🏹🚫
• **Go to the beach**: A seaside visit. (Related: Vacation). 🏖️

question: Q.13 A **'bitter pill to swallow'** is:
explanation:
• **Unpleasant fact**: A fact or event that is disagreeable but must be accepted. (Related: Harsh reality). 💊😣
• **Medicine**: Substance used for treatment. (Literal meaning). 🩺

question: Q.14 If you **'spill the beans'**, you:
explanation:
• **Reveal secret**: To let out information that was supposed to be kept private. (Related: Let the cat out of the bag). 🫘🗣️
• **Waste food**: Throwing away edible items. (Related: Fritter away). 🗑️

question: Q.15 To **'blow one's top'** means to:
explanation:
• **Very angry**: To lose one's temper suddenly. (Related: Go ballistic, Hit the roof). 🌋😡
• **Wear a hat**: To place headwear. (Literal meaning). 🎩

question: Q.16 **'Playing second fiddle'** means having:
explanation:
• **Subordinate role**: To be in a less important or less powerful position than someone else. (Related: Underling). 🎻🥈
• **A lead role**: Being the most important person. (Antonym: Protagonist). 🥇

question: Q.17 To **'jump the gun'** means to:
explanation:
• **Too early**: To do something before the appropriate or designated time. (Related: Premature). 🔫🏃
• **Win a race**: Being the fastest. (Related: Victorious). 🏁

question: Q.18 Keeping something **'under wraps'** means keeping it:
explanation:
• **Secret**: Keeping information concealed from the public. (Related: Classified). 🎁🤫
• **Cold**: At a low temperature. (Literal meaning). ❄️

question: Q.19 A **'skeleton in the closet'** is an:
explanation:
• **Embarrassing secret**: A shocking or shameful fact about someone that they want to keep hidden. (Related: Scandal). 💀🚪
• **Unfinished task**: Work that is not yet completed. (Related: Backlog). 📝

question: Q.20 To **'face the music'** means to:
explanation:
• **Accept consequences**: To receive punishment or criticism for something you have done. (Related: Pay the piper). 🎶⚖️
• **Ignore criticism**: To disregard negative feedback. (Antonym). 🙉

question: Q.21 **'Walking on eggshells'** implies being:
explanation:
• **Extremely cautious**: Being very careful not to offend or upset someone. (Related: Treading carefully). 🥚👣
• **Very clumsy**: Lacking grace or skill in movement. (Antonym: Dexterous). 🧱

question: Q.22 A **'chip on one's shoulder'** is a sign of:
explanation:
• **Holding a grudge**: Having an angry or resentful attitude because of a past grievance. (Related: Resentment). 🪵😤
• **Being strong**: Having physical or mental power. (Related: Robust). 💪

question: Q.23 **'Biting off more than you can chew'** means:
options: Taking on a task too difficult
answer: Taking on a task too difficult
explanation:
• **Task too difficult**: Trying to do something that is beyond your ability or capacity. (Related: Overextending). 🦷🍔
• **Eating too much**: Overindulging in food. (Related: Gluttony). 🥘

question: Q.24 To **'bark up the wrong tree'** is to:
options: Follow a mistaken line of thought
answer: Follow a mistaken line of thought
explanation:
• **Mistaken thought**: To be wrong about the reason for something or the way to achieve something. (Related: Misguided). 🌳🐕
• **Get lost**: Losing one's way. (Related: Disoriented). 🗺️

question: Q.25 To **'hit the nail on the head'** means to:
explanation:
• **Exactly right**: To identify exactly what the problem or answer is. (Related: Precision, Spot on). 🔨🎯
• **Cause an accident**: Resulting in unintended harm. (Related: Mishap). 💥

question: Q.26 Being **'on the fence'** means you are:
explanation:
• **Undecided**: Being unable or unwilling to make a decision between two options. (Related: Wavering). 🚧🤔
• **Very sure**: Having complete confidence. (Antonym: Certainty). ✅

question: Q.27 To **'see eye to eye'** with someone is to:
explanation:
• **Agree fully**: To have exactly the same opinion as someone else. (Related: In accord). 👁️🤝
• **Fight them**: Engaging in conflict. (Antonym: At loggerheads). ⚔️

question: Q.28 To **'give the benefit of the doubt'** is to:
explanation:
• **Trust without proof**: To believe something good about someone even though you are not sure. (Related: Credulity). ⚖️❓
• **Be suspicious**: Lacking trust in someone. (Antonym). 🤨

question: Q.29 To **'go the extra mile'** means to:
explanation:
• **More than expected**: To make a special effort to achieve something or help someone. (Related: Surpassing). 🏃🏽‍♂️➕
• **Be tired**: Lacking energy. (Related: Exhausted). 🥱

question: Q.30 To **'let the cat out of the bag'** is to:
explanation:
• **Reveal secret**: To tell a secret by mistake or carelessly. (Related: Spill the beans). 🐈🛍️
• **Release a pet**: Setting an animal free. (Literal meaning). 🌲
